patches.hypothesis: The bug is likely due to the sum of squared distances ('sum') being reset for each point, preventing proper probability computation for initial center selection.
patches.changes:
- Change type of 'sum' to double.
- Correctly compute squared distances without resetting 'sum'.
- Calculate distance as squared value once per point.
patches.diff:

patches.hypothesis: The bug is caused by an incorrect calculation of the sum of squared distances. Instead of calculating the cumulative sum for each data point, we should be calculating the individual squared distance and use that for probability proportional selection.
patches.fixed_method:
<T extends Clusterable<T>> List<Cluster<T>> chooseInitialCenters(final Collection<T> points, final int k, final Random random) {

    final List<T> pointSet = new ArrayList<T>(points);
    final List<Cluster<T>> resultSet = new ArrayList<Cluster<T>>();

    // Choose one center uniformly at random from among the data points.
    final T firstPoint = pointSet.remove(random.nextInt(pointSet.size()));
    resultSet.add(new Cluster<T>(firstPoint));

    final double[] dx2 = new double[pointSet.size()];
    while (resultSet.size() < k) {
        // For each data point x, compute D(x), the distance between x and
        // the nearest center that has already been chosen.
        double sum = 0.0;
        for (int i = 0; i < pointSet.size(); i++) {
            final T p = pointSet.get(i);
            final Cluster<T> nearest = getNearestCluster(resultSet, p);
            final double d = p.distanceFrom(nearest.getCenter());
            dx2[i] = d * d;
            sum += dx2[i];
        }

        // Add one new data point as a center. Each point x is chosen with
        // probability proportional to D(x)^2
        final double r = random.nextDouble() * sum;
        for (int i = 0; i < dx2.length; i++) {
            if (dx2[i] >= r) {
                final T p = pointSet.remove(i);
                resultSet.add(new Cluster<T>(p));
                break;
            }
        }
    }

    return resultSet;
}

patches.hypothesis: The bug is caused by incorrect computation of probabilities due to cumulative sum in dx2. This makes it miss selecting points closer to and around unique points.
patches.changes: int sum = 0; -> double sum = 0; changed cumulative sum to separate d variable
patches.fixed_method:
<T extends Clusterable<T>> List<Cluster<T>> chooseInitialCenters(final Collection<T> points, final int k, final Random random) {

    final List<T> pointSet = new ArrayList<T>(points);
    final List<Cluster<T>> resultSet = new ArrayList<Cluster<T>>();

    // Choose one center uniformly at random from among the data points.
    final T firstPoint = pointSet.remove(random.nextInt(pointSet.size()));
    resultSet.add(new Cluster<T>(firstPoint));

    final double[] dx2 = new double[pointSet.size()];
    while (resultSet.size() < k) {
        // For each data point x, compute D(x), the distance between x and
        // the nearest center that has already been chosen.
        double sum = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < pointSet.size(); i++) {
            final T p = pointSet.get(i);
            final Cluster<T> nearest = getNearestCluster(resultSet, p);
            final double d = p.distanceFrom(nearest.getCenter());
            dx2[i] = d * d;
            sum += dx2[i];
        }

        // Add one new data point as a center. Each point x is chosen with
        // probability proportional to D(x)^2
        final double r = random.nextDouble() * sum;
        double cumulativeProbability = 0;
        for (int i = 0 ; i < dx2.length; i++) {
            cumulativeProbability += dx2[i];
            if (cumulativeProbability >= r) {
                final T p = pointSet.remove(i);
                resultSet.add(new Cluster<T>(p));
                break;
            }
        }
    }

    return resultSet;

}

patches.hypothesis: The `dx2` array is used incorrectly which accumulates distances instead of storing the incremental distance for each point. This results in incorrect probabilistic selection of new centers based on distance.
patches.changes:
- Change array dx2 to store individual squared distances instead of cumulative distances
- Correctly use these distances for randomized selection
patches.fixed_method:
<T extends Clusterable<T>> List<Cluster<T>> chooseInitialCenters(final Collection<T> points, final int k, final Random random) {

    final List<T> pointSet = new ArrayList<T>(points);
    final List<Cluster<T>> resultSet = new ArrayList<Cluster<T>>();

    // Choose one center uniformly at random from among the data points.
    final T firstPoint = pointSet.remove(random.nextInt(pointSet.size()));
    resultSet.add(new Cluster<T>(firstPoint));

    final double[] dx2 = new double[pointSet.size()];
    while (resultSet.size() < k) {
        double sum = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < pointSet.size(); i++) {
            final T p = pointSet.get(i);
            final Cluster<T> nearest = getNearestCluster(resultSet, p);
            final double d = p.distanceFrom(nearest.getCenter());
            dx2[i] = d * d;
            sum += dx2[i];
        }

        // Add one new data point as a center. Each point x is chosen with
        // probability proportional to D(x)^2
        final double r = random.nextDouble() * sum;
        double cumsum = 0;
        for (int i = 0 ; i < dx2.length; i++) {
            cumsum += dx2[i];
            if (cumsum >= r) {
                final T p = pointSet.remove(i);
                resultSet.add(new Cluster<T>(p));
                break;
            }
        }
    }

    return resultSet;

}
patches.diff:
- Change array dx2 to store individual squared distances instead of cumulative distances
- Correctly use these distances for randomized selection

patches.hypothesis: The cumulative sum dx2 array is being updated incorrectly, causing biased selection of centers.
patches.fixed_method:
<T extends Clusterable<T>> List<Cluster<T>> chooseInitialCenters(final Collection<T> points, final int k, final Random random) {
    final List<T> pointSet = new ArrayList<T>(points);
    final List<Cluster<T>> resultSet = new ArrayList<Cluster<T>>();

    // Choose one center uniformly at random from among the data points.
    final T firstPoint = pointSet.remove(random.nextInt(pointSet.size()));
    resultSet.add(new Cluster<T>(firstPoint));

    final double[] dx2 = new double[pointSet.size()];
    while (resultSet.size() < k) {
        // For each data point x, compute D(x), the distance between x and
        // the nearest center that has already been chosen.
        for (int i = 0; i < pointSet.size(); i++) {
            final T p = pointSet.get(i);
            final Cluster<T> nearest = getNearestCluster(resultSet, p);
            final double d = p.distanceFrom(nearest.getCenter());
            dx2[i] = d * d;
        }

        // Compute cumulative sum
        for (int i = 1; i < dx2.length; i++) {
            dx2[i] += dx2[i - 1];
        }

        // Add one new data point as a center. Each point x is chosen with
        // probability proportional to D(x)^2
        final double r = random.nextDouble() * dx2[dx2.length - 1];
        for (int i = 0; i < dx2.length; i++) {
            if (dx2[i] >= r) {
                final T p = pointSet.remove(i);
                resultSet.add(new Cluster<T>(p));
                break;
            }
        }
    }
    return resultSet;
}

patches.hypothesis: The bug is caused by incorrectly accumulating squared distances, which causes incorrect selection of the unique point as a center when distances are small.

- Recalculate cumulative probabilities correctly.
